titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of NRS GD55/136

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=407 status=live modified=2021-04-06 15:03:46.543504+00 | translation moa=408 status=live modified=2021-04-06 15:03:48.080136+00

p.span:
Omnibus sancte Matris Ecclesie filiis presentibus et futuris Ricardus . de Lincolnia Salutem . Sciatis me dedisse et concessisse
et hac mea Carta confirmasse deo et Ecclesie Sancte marie de Melros et Monachis ibidem deo seruientibus pro
animabus patris mei et matris mee et Omnium antecessorum et successorum meorum . et pro salute domini mei Willelmi Regis et Re
gine et Alexandri filii sui et Comitis . dauid . et pro salute mea et uxoris mee et Omnium heredum nostrorum in puram et perpetu
am elemosinam illam partem terre in territorio de Molle quam Ansel de Wittune dedit eidem Ecclesie et monachis cum
pratis adiacentibus tenendam et possidendam in perpetuum per omnes terminos et diuisas que continentur in Carta predicti Anselmi .
preterea dedi eis et confirmaui totam illam petariam que est inter Molope et Berope et Herdstrete que diuidit terram de Mol
le et de Hunum . Insuper dedi eis et confirmaui in bosco meo de Molope buschiam quantum equus unus potest portare
usque ad grangiam de Hunedun singulis annis a pascha usque ad Natiuitatem Sancte Marie . Volo itaque ut predicti
monachi teneant et possideant hec omnia in perpetuum ita libere et quiete plenarie et honorifice sicut aliquam aliam ele
mosinam liberius et quietius plenius et honorificentius tenent et possident . Et ego et heredes mei manutenebimus et warantizabi
mus predictis monachis hanc elemosinam contra omnes homines et adquietabimus eam de forensic et Omni terreno seruitio erga
omnes homines in perpetuum . Et ipsi monachi habebunt liberum et congruum ingressum et egressum per terram meam ad ducendam
buschiam suam et petas et alia sibi neccessaria sine disturbatione et molestia . Hiis T’. Geruasio Auenel . Willelmo . auenel .
W . de Cresseuill’ . W. de Cracin . Alano clerico . Walter . de Ridale . Roberto . de Bernoluebi .

div.p: To all sons of Holy Mother church, present and future, Richard of Lincoln sends greeting. Know me to have given and granted and by this my charter to have made firm to God and to the church of St Mary at Melrose and to the monks serving God in that place, for the souls of my father and my mother and of all my ancestors and successors, and for the salvation of my lord, King William, and of the queen and their son Alexander, and of Earl David, and for my own salvation and my wife's and all our heirs' salvation, in pure and everlasting alms, the portion of land in the territory of Mow that Anselm of Whitton gave to the same church and monks with the adjacent meadows, to be held and possessed for ever by all the borders and boundaries contained in the charter of the said Anslem. In addition I have given and confirmed the whole peatery that is between Mowhope and Berhope and 'Herdstrete' which divides the land of Mow and of Hownam. I have moreover given them in my wood of Mowhope as much firewood as one horse can carry to Hownam Grange, each year from Easter to the Nativity of Saint Mary. I will that the said monks hold and possess all these things for ever as freely and quitely and honourably as they hold and possess any other alms freely and quitely, fully and honourably. And I and my heirs shall maintain and warrant to the said monks this alms against all men, and dishcarge it of forinsec and every earthly service in respect of all men for ever. And the monks shall have free and appropriate entrance and exit through my land for fetching their firewood and peats and other necessities without disturbance and hindrance. With these witnesses: Gervase Avenel; William Avenel; William of Cresswell; William de Crachin; Alan the clerk; Walter of Ryedale; Robert of Barnoldby.
